decision-tree.root-question: Is AI the delivery mechanism (not a bolt-on feature)?

branch.question: For each AI component: model_id pinned AND risk_tier classified AND build-vs-buy scored AND inference cost measured?

branch.when: model_id_pinned == true AND risk_tier_classified == true AND build_vs_buy_scored == true AND inference_cost_measured == true
branch.conclusion: Emit the roadmap. Wire the human-refines UX with telemetry; expose inference cost on the product dashboard.
Block. Replace "latest" with `model-name@YYYY-MM-DD` for every component before commit.
Block. Classify each component against EU AI Act tiers before committing the roadmap.
Block. Score core-differentiator + unique-data-advantage for every build decision; set a 12-18 month revisit date.
Block. Measure inference cost per active user per day for every component before commit.
Route to traditional PM methodology. This methodology only fires when AI IS the mechanism, not a feature.

example.p: Design suite where AI generates layouts. Components: prototype-generator + voice-clone. Both pinned (opus-4.6@2026-04-01), risk-tier limited, build (core differentiator + design corpus), inference $0.12/active-user-day, refines UX wired. → conclusion r1-human-refines-as-feature.
